evals.description: Trigger test: activates on a classic system design interview question
evals.prompt: Design a URL shortener like bit.ly. Walk me through the architecture.

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on CAP theorem trade-off question
evals.prompt: We're building a distributed inventory system for an e-commerce site. How should we think about CAP theorem when choosing our database?

evals.description: Core task: designing a rate limiter with correct algorithm recommendation
evals.prompt: I need to build a rate limiter for our public API. We want to allow bursts but enforce a per-user hourly limit. What algorithm should I use and how do I implement it in a distributed environment?

evals.description: Core task: designing a notification system with correct queue isolation
evals.prompt: Design a notification system that can send push notifications, emails, and SMS. How do you handle different delivery speeds and third-party provider outages?

evals.description: Core task: SQL vs NoSQL selection with correct reasoning
evals.prompt: We're building a social media platform. We need to store user profiles, posts, and follower relationships. What database or databases should we use?

evals.description: Core task: back-of-envelope capacity estimation with correct process
evals.prompt: We expect 10 million DAU for our new photo sharing app. Each user uploads 2 photos per day on average. Each photo is 2 MB. How much storage do we need for the first year?

evals.description: Core task: designing a chat system with correct protocol and storage choices
evals.prompt: Design a real-time group chat system like Slack. It needs to support millions of concurrent connections and message history.

evals.description: Core task: caching strategy with correct invalidation approach
evals.prompt: Our product catalog is read millions of times a day but updated only a few times per hour. We want to cache it in Redis. What caching pattern should we use and how do we handle invalidation?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against sharding before exhausting simpler scaling options
evals.prompt: Our PostgreSQL database is getting slow under load. Should we shard it across multiple nodes?

evals.description: Gotcha: correctly identifies SPOF when a naive single-LB design is proposed
evals.prompt: We're putting a single load balancer in front of our three app servers. Our CTO says this is good enough for now. What risks should we flag?

evals.description: Gotcha: catches inconsistency when shared database between microservices is proposed
evals.prompt: We have an orders service and a payments service. Our team wants them to share the same PostgreSQL database to keep things simple. Is that OK?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not claim CA systems exist in distributed context
evals.prompt: My architect says we should design our distributed database to be both fully consistent and fully available at all times. Is that achievable?

assertions.value: both consistency and availability are always possible

evals.description: References load: points to interview framework reference for interview prep
evals.prompt: I have a system design interview at a FAANG company next week. Can you walk me through how to structure my answer during the interview?
